mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, reducing the present value of equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns is greater than or equal to 0.0 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4% signals the onset of a recessionary contraction in labor demand, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return following UNRATE above 4% is not significantly negative (t-test one-sided p > 0.10 relative to non-triggered periods).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high CPI inflation and high federal funds rate compress equity valuations through both elevated discount rates and squeezed profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of CPIAUCSL above 300 and FEDFUNDS above 4% produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return during periods of positive month-over-month INDPRO growth is not significantly greater than zero (t-test one-sided p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1400 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return following HOUST below 1400 is not negative (mean >= 0).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously strong payroll employment growth and very low unemployment indicate an overheating labor market that pressures wages and corporate margins, ultimately weighing on S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of PAYEMS above 155000 and UNRATE below 3.5% produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail and food services sales reflect robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS level and 60-day forward return is less than 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI year-over-year inflation exceeding 3% triggers market expectations of tighter monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Proportion of negative 20-day returns after CPI Y/Y exceeds 3% is not significantly greater than 0.5 (binomial test p > 0.05).
